rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical [3+2] cycloaddition of styrenes with nitromethane to form 4,5-dihydroisoxazole N-oxides (oximes) via in situ generation of a 1,2,5-oxadiazole 2-oxide intermediate
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of iodide (n-Bu4NI) to I2/I., which mediates HAT from deprotonated nitromethane (CH2=NO2-, intermediate II) to generate fulminate-like HC≡N+-O- (intermediate III); cathode regenerates iodide and produces H2
electron_flow: Anode oxidizes iodide to I./I2, which abstracts H from the t-BuOK-deprotonated nitromethane (II) via HAT to give the nitrile-oxide-like intermediate III; two molecules of III undergo self [3+2] cycloaddition to furoxan IV, which then performs a [3+2] cycloaddition with styrene to afford the dihydroisoxazole N-oxide product. Cathode reduces protons to H2 and regenerates I-.
rate_determining_step: HAT from intermediate II (aci-nitromethane) by iodine radical to form nitrile oxide III (inverse KIE ~0.74 observed, consistent with sp3→sp rehybridization at the HAT step)

probe_reagents.name: styrene-d8
probe_reagents.observation: 2a-d8 isolated in 87-91% yield; deuterium fully retained in product, confirming styrene is incorporated intact via [3+2]

probe_reagents.name: CD3NO2 / CH3NO2 competition
probe_reagents.observation: kH/kD = 0.74 (avg of 4 runs: 0.72, 0.75, 0.72, 0.75), inverse secondary KIE consistent with C-H/D cleavage at nitromethane and sp3→sp rehybridization

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Dihydroisoxazole product 4e isolated in 60%; a 1,1-diphenyl-2-CF3 alkene byproduct detected by HRMS, indicating .CF3 radical (from CF3CO2-) is generated and trapped

probe_reagents.name: litmus paper / limewater headspace test
probe_reagents.observation: Litmus turns red and limewater becomes turbid over time, indicating CO2 evolution (from Kolbe-type decarboxylation of CF3CO2-) and acidic gas release

probe_reagents.name: HRMS detection of furoxan IV
probe_reagents.observation: ESI-HRMS m/z (M+Na)+ calcd 109.0008, obsd 109.0009, confirming 1,2,5-oxadiazole 2-oxide IV is present in the reaction mixture; trapping with styrene after electrolysis gave 2a in 25-30% yield

notes: DFT compared three HAT pathways from II to III: by I. (ΔH‡ = 51.3 kJ/mol, most favorable), by I2 (94.8 kJ/mol), and by .CF3 (79.1 kJ/mol), supporting iodine-radical-mediated HAT as the operative pathway. Self [3+2] of two III to furoxan IV has ΔH‡ ≈ 112 kJ/mol; subsequent [3+2] of IV with styrene has ΔH‡ ≈ 109 kJ/mol. Alternative direct nitrile-oxide + alkene [3+2] without furoxan intermediate is disfavored relative to the observed pathway through IV. CF3CO2Na acts as electrolyte and undergoes some Kolbe decarboxylation (CO2 + .CF3 observed) but .CF3 is not the dominant HAT agent.
